description_long: This is a traditional djembe drum with a goblet-shaped body made from reddish-brown wood, featuring a series of horizontal decorative grooves and dotted patterns around its midsection. The drumhead is made of taut, light-colored animal skin, tightly secured with a complex network of dark rope lacing that forms a crisscross pattern around the upper body. The rope tensioning system is knotted at several points, ensuring the drumhead remains firmly in place for optimal sound resonance. The base of the drum flares outward, providing stability when placed on the ground. The surface of the drum body appears matte and slightly textured, while the drumhead is smooth and slightly soft to the touch. This type of drum is commonly used in street performances, parks, and community gatherings in urban environments.
description: A goblet-shaped djembe drum with a reddish-brown wooden body, light animal skin drumhead, and dark rope lacing. Decorative grooves and a flared base add to its traditional look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full drumhead, intricate rope lacing, and decorative grooves on the wooden body.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the rope knots and the curvature of the drum's base.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the symmetry of the rope lacing and the even distribution of decorative patterns.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the rope tensioning system and the flared base's stability.
